title: Warfare and Military Operations
description: RAND researchers examine military and national security issues across a broad spectrum—from political dissent and military training to tactical operations and reconstruction efforts—and take a long-term, global perspective. Terrorism, types of warfare, and international intervention are among the many topics RAND explores.
text:
Pursuing Disarmament, Demobilization, and Reintegration in Gaza: A Critical Pathway to a Durable Peace
The authors propose a disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR) framework for Gaza. They also describe the preconditions necessary for a successful DDR program in Gaza, including the link to deradicalization.
How Israel’s War in Gaza (Partially) Rehabilitated Counterinsurgency Theory
Hamas’s Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attack on Israel—and the ensuing war in Gaza—have prompted a renewed reckoning with population-centric counterinsurgency strategy. If such conflicts are inevitable, then what is the “least bad” way to fight them?
New START’s Expiration Will Make the World Less Safe—Even if It Doesn’t Spark Another Nuclear Arms Race
The expiration of the New START treaty won't trigger an immediate arms race. But without the norms, transparency, and dialogues that have helped manage nuclear competition for decades, the strategic environment will be more dangerous for everyone.
Improving Interoperability with Allies and Partners: Aircraft Maintenance and Base Operating Support
The U.S. Air Force must generate and sustain airpower from dispersed, partner-owned locations. This brief discusses interoperability not as an end state but as a spectrum of cooperation that is tailored to requirements, standards, and capabilities.
Exploring Duality in Space: How Select Countries Approach Dual-Use Space Systems
In this report, the authors present the findings of a literature review in which they examined the landscape of national approaches to dual-use space systems across select countries in the Americas, Europe-Eurasia, and the Indo-Pacific.
The Israel-Iran Détente Won't Last
Netanyahu's deal with Tehran was more a tactical pause than a game changer in the ongoing rivalry. The hiatus is unlikely to last long, and there are good reasons to believe that the next round of the Iran-Israel war will be even bigger than before.
Contemporary Foundation AI Models Increase Biological Weapons Risk
Examining contemporary foundation artificial intelligence (AI) models, the authors show that AI models can successfully guide users through the technical processes required to develop biological weapons, which might increase the risk of their use.
In Ukraine, Is the U.S. Promising Too Much?
Ukraine will need security guarantees as part of any deal to end the war with Russia. But expansive U.S. commitments may do little to deter future aggression by Moscow—and pose risks for Kyiv, Washington, and U.S. allies in Europe.
A Real Negotiation Process
President Trump has focused global attention on ending the Russia-Ukraine war, but a key piece of the puzzle is still missing: a real negotiation process. Structured, confidential talks are needed to break the deadlock and build trust to give peace a chance.
